entry.title: SAP Advanced Financial Closing: Third-Party Integration
entry.content:
Dear Finance Experts,

With the latest major release 6.0 of SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC) released on February 15, 2026, SAP introduces an important new capability: Third-Party System Integration.

This feature enables external systems to participate natively in AFC closing processes by acting as scheduling providers. It significantly expands AFC’s integration scope and allows customers to orchestrate financial close activities beyond SAP-managed execution engines.

Background

SAP Advanced Financial Closing has long been the strategic and central orchestration layer for financial close activities, coordinating tasks, dependencies, and monitoring across complex landscapes. With Third-Party System Integration, SAP formalizes this extensibility by introducing a public Scheduling Provider Interface. This allows external systems to:

  • Receive job execution requests from AFC

  • Report job execution status and results

  • Participate fully in AFC process monitoring and error handling

AFC remains the single point of orchestration, while execution can happen anywhere across the enterprise assuming that the Third-Party System Integration is properly enabled and set-up.

Getting Started

At a high level, integrating a third-party system with AFC consists of three steps:

  1. Implement the AFC Scheduling Provider Interface
    Your system exposes REST endpoints that comply with the AFC scheduling contract.

  2. Register the provider in AFC
    The provider is configured in AFC as a communication system capable of executing tasks.

  3. Use AFC Closing Templates
    Closing tasks are modeled as usual in AFC, but assigned to the external provider.

SAP provides all necessary specifications and tooling via the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and GitHub.

Scheduling Provider Interface

The SAP Advanced Financial Closing Scheduling Provider Interface defines the protocol between AFC and an external communication system.

Key characteristics:

  • REST-based API

  • Clearly defined lifecycle operations

  • Status and result reporting back to AFC

Typical responsibilities of a 3rd-party scheduling provider include:

  • Accepting task execution requests as jobs

  • Starting and monitoring jobs

  • Handling cancellations

  • Returning job results and messages

The complete API specification is available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub:

  • SAP Advanced Financial Closing Scheduling Provider Interface

This specification is the definitive source and should be followed strictly to ensure compatibility with AFC.

SAP Advanced Financial Closing SDK for CDS

To simplify implementation, SAP provides the SAP Advanced Financial Closing SDK for CDS, published as an open-source project.

The Software Development Kit (SDK)

  • Is based on the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP)

  • Provides ready-to-use service definitions

  • Includes data models aligned with AFC semantics

  • Reduces boilerplate and integration effort

Using the SDK, developers can focus on:

  • Mapping AFC task executions to external job logic

  • Implementing business-specific processing

  • Handling system-specific scheduling and monitoring

The SDK architecture is summarized in the following diagram:

The SDK is available on GitHub and all the details can be found there:

  • SAP Advanced Financial Closing SDK for CDS

This makes it especially attractive for cloud-native integrations and rapid prototyping based on the SAP Business Technology Platform.

Hands-On

The following section shows in detail how to build a Third-Party Scheduling Provider implementing the Scheduling Provider Interface with the SAP Advanced Financial Closing SDK for CDS.


Prerequisites 

Setup Node.js: https://nodejs.org/en/download

Install CDS module globally

npm install -g @sap/cds


Step 1: Create AFC SDK based CAP Project

Start with a standard CAP Node.js project:
cds init scheduler --nodejs
cd scheduler

Add the AFC SDK dependency:

npm install @cap-js-community/sap-afc-sdk

Note: This blog leverages the CAP Node.js runtime. As the AFC SDK is also fully compatible with CAP Java runtime, third-party scheduling provider applications can also be based on a CAP Java projects, following the details in the SAP SDK landing page CAP Java section.


Step 2: Start Project

Start the generated project:

npm start

Open the application URL in browser: http://localhost:4004.

A welcome page is displayed giving an overview of the out-of-the-box functionality provided.

Specifically, the Scheduling Provider API implementing the Scheduling Provider Interface is served already at http://localhost:4004/api/job-scheduling/v1.

There is no content yet.

Step 3: Add Job Definitions

Install AFC SDK Command-Line-Interface (CLI)

npm install -g @cap-js-community/sap-afc-sdk

Add basic sample data for testing:

afc add sample -m

Project is extended to include CSV files with sample job definitions placed at /db/data.

Restart the generated project:

npm start

The Scheduling Provider API exposes automatically the job definitions at http://localhost:4004/api/job-scheduling/v1/JobDefinition (for example,JOB_1) and the corresponding parameters, for example, for  JOB_1 at http://localhost:4004/api/job-scheduling/v1/JobDefinition/JOB_1/parameters.

The CSV data can be adjusted according to the specific use-case. 

Step 4: Implement Job Logic

Add implementation stub to project:

afc add stub

Empty implementation files are placed at /srv:

  • srv/scheduling-processing-service.js: Hooks to implement custom logic for the job processing (create, update, cancel, sync, and so on) on processing level

  • srv/scheduling-provider-service.js: Hooks to implement custom logic for job creation and job cancellation on provider service level

Edit processJob hook in file srv/scheduling-processing-service.js to enhance the logic to automatically complete the job successfully after 5 seconds:

this.on(processJob, async (req, next) => {
  await this.triggerJobUpdate(req, req.job, JobStatus.completed, [{
    type: ResultType.message,
    name: "Message",
    messages: [{
      code: "jobCompleted",
      severity: MessageSeverity.info
    }],
  }], new Date(Date.now() + 5000)); // 5 seconds
  await next(); // set job status to running
});

Constants can be imported from AFC SDK as follows:

const {ResultType, MessageSeverity, JobStatus} = require("@cap-js-community/sap-afc-sdk");

The job processing logic can be adjusted according to the specific use-case.The idea is that the job processing is forwarded to the respective external job execution endpoint and that status updates are reported back asynchronously via this.processJobUpdate. Here this is simulated by a timer-based job update trigger.

Restart the generated project:

npm start

A new job instance now can be generated for, example, via curl:

curl -u alice:alice -X POST "http://localhost:4004/api/job-scheduling/v1/Job" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "JOB_1",
    "referenceID": "b001397c-b7f9-4f03-ba79-ec11297cae81",
    "parameters": [
      {
        "name": "A",
        "value": "abc"
      },
      {
        "name": "B",
        "value": 21
      },
      {
        "name": "C",
        "value": false
      }
    ]
  }'

The instance created returns the job ID with the initial status requested. Processing starts and shall update the job from status running to completed, when fetching the instance:

curl -u alice:alice -X GET "http://localhost:4004/api/job-scheduling/v1/Job/<jobID>"

With job status completed, the results of the job execution can be fetched via:

curl -u alice:alice -X GET "http://localhost:4004/api/job-scheduling/v1/Job/<jobID>/results"

Likewise result messages can be fetched via:

curl -u alice:alice -X GET "http://localhost:4004/api/job-scheduling/v1/JobResult/<jobResultID>/messages"

As alternative to curl, http files can be added (for example, at http/scheduling/provider.local.http)  to execute http requests against the local Scheduling Provider API, via command:

afc add http


Step 5: Add Security

The application must be secured before productive usage. Respective guidance can be found in CAP documentation: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/security. Choose between XSUAA-based or IAS-based authentication and authorization to secure the APIs. In case of XSUAA, AFC SDK supports the creation of a Service Broker via command:

afc add broker​


Step 6: Deploy to Cloud

Project shall be prepared for deployment using the AFC SDK CLI:

Cloud Foundry: 

afc init cf

Kyma: 

afc init kyma

Additional guidance on cloud deployments can be found in CAP documentation: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/deployment.

After successful deployment, the external scheduling provider is available via an internet-facing endpoint that can be reached by SAP Advanced Financial Closing through the SAP Destination Service.

Step 7: Create Destination

A destination to the external scheduling provider needs to be configured in the SAP BTP Cockpit on sub-account level in the Connectivity/Destinations section. The concrete destination configuration highly depends on the target application configuration.

An example destination configuration looks as follows (for example, OAuth2ClientCredentials)

destination:
  Name: "external-scheduler"
  tokenServiceURLType: "Dedicated"
  Type: "HTTP"
  clientId: "<client-id>"
  Authentication: "OAuth2ClientCredentials"
  clientSecret: "<client-secret>"
  tokenServiceURL: "https://authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token"
  ProxyType: "Internet"
  URL: "https://<url>/api/job-scheduling/v1"
  Description: "External Scheduler"

Fill the security-relevant configurations (clientId, clientSecret, tokenServiceURL, URL) from the client secret (IAS) or service key (XSUAA broker) from the specific setup of the scheduling provider application.

Step 8: Create Communication System

With the destination ready, a communication system of system type External System can be created in the SAP Advanced Financial Closing Specify Communication System app:


Step 9: Use External System in Template 

In the Manage Closing Task Lists app, you can include jobs from external communication system into the folder structure using the  Create Task from External Job button. A task list is generated as usual to process the external jobs.


Step 10: Process external jobs

External jobs integrated from external third-party systems can be processed as any other job-like task as part of the SAP Advanced Financial Closing scheduler and status and job results are synchronized afterwards:


With this implementation, the third-party system integration is fully demonstrated, proving that external jobs can be reliably processed within the automated scheduling framework of SAP Advanced Financial Closing.

Summary

SAP Advanced Financial Closing 6.0 introduces Third-Party System Integration, enabling external schedulers and execution engines to participate seamlessly in financial close processes. With the Scheduling Provider Interface and the AFC SDK, developers can quickly build compliant providers, manage job execution, and report statuses back to AFC. This new capability allows organizations to extend AFC beyond SAP-managed systems, integrate custom or cloud-native engines, and maintain centralized orchestration—all while reducing development complexity and ensuring reliable monitoring.

Thank you for taking the time and reading this blog post.

For more information on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, check out the following links:

  • SAP Community Page for SAP Advanced Financial Closing: here
  • SAP Help Portal for AFC: here
  • SAP Help Administration Guide - Third-Party Systems: here
  • SAP API Business Hub (SSPI): here
  • AFC in the SAP Roadmap Explorer: here
  • SAP.com page about AFC: here


Follow us via @SAP on twitter and tag #AdvancedFinancialClosing, or myself via @OliverKlemenz.

Disclaimer

Please note that SAP Advanced Financial Closing gets frequent updates and enhancements. Therefore, the current features and functions may not be exactly as described here. For the latest information, you can refer to the product documentation on the SAP Help Portal.

entry.title: Localization in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
In today's interconnected world, running a global business can feel like navigating a vast ocean. Each country is a new port with its own unique set of rules, regulations, and customs. With the 2602 release, we're not just giving you a better map; we're upgrading your entire navigation system, ensuring you can sail smoothly and confidently, no matter the destination.

Let’s dive into the exciting new features that will help you go global while staying perfectly local.

  • A Single Source of Truth: The Intuitive Tax Register
  • Mastering Global Complexity: Country-Specific Highlights
  • Universal Innovations for Global Business
  • AI campaigns

A Single Source of Truth: The Intuitive Tax Register

Imagine a world where your tax data isn't scattered across different ledgers and reports, but lives in one, centralized, and transparent place. This world is here with the new Tax Register for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. This is a true game changer for tax accountants.

We are providing a single source of truth for all your indirect tax operations. This powerful tool allows you to:

1) Visualize the entire lifecycle of each tax line, from posting to reporting and payment.
2) Gain centralized insights and a real-time view of your tax position, company wide.
3) Increase flexibility by making tax operations independent from accounting.
4) Effortlessly reconcile and ensure you are always audit-ready.

Initially rolling out for VAT in Germany, Belgium, the Netherlands, and Oman, the Tax Register is your new command center for managing tax payables and ensuring comprehensive compliance.

  • Display Tax Items (ID: F4756):
    • View and analyze posted tax items with tax-specific attributes.
    • Track the lifecycle of tax items.
    • Navigate to related documents for detailed insights.

Display Tax Items (ID: F4756)

  • Manage Tax Payable (ID: F4755)
    • Create, post, and cancel tax payable documents.
    • Automatically generate documents when reports reach Content Accepted by Government status.
    • Option to manually create tax payable documents if required.

Manage Tax Payable (ID: F4755)

  • Migrate Tax Items(ID: F6715): Migrate tax items posted prior to Tax Register activation from the standard database table (BSET) to the new table (TAX_REG_ITEM) for reporting and analysis.

 

   Migrate Tax Items (ID: F6715)

Mastering Global Complexity: Country-Specific Highlights

Every region has its own complexities, and our 2602 release delivers targeted solutions to make your life easier.

Turkey: Taming Foreign Exchange Gains

Navigating the nuances of foreign currency in Turkey just got a whole lot simpler. Businesses know the challenge of the (Kur fark) foreign exchange gain invoice required when exchange rates shift between invoicing and payment. Our update automates the creation of billing documents for these gains in Convergent Invoicing. The system automatically calculates the gain, ensures correct VAT treatment, and generates the necessary invoices and accounting documents, saving you invaluable time and minimizing errors.
(Kur fark) foreign exchange gains

Brazil: Unprecedented Flexibility and Extensibility

Brazil's fiscal landscape is famously dynamic, and we're putting more power than ever into your hands. This release introduces a suite of enhancements for Brazil, including:

a. Self-Service for External Tax Engines: A new configuration interface allows you to map custom tax type codes to an external tax engine, enhancing flexibility in sales processing.
b. Extensible Nota Fiscal Models: You can now extend and adapt nota fiscal models for sales processes, allowing you to meet specific requirements not covered by the standard version.
c. Multiple Account Assignments: We now enable external tax calculation for multiple account assignment distribution during invoice verification, enhancing customer satisfaction and accuracy.

Universal Innovations for Global Business

Compliance is a global theme, and some of our most powerful new features are designed to work across borders.

1) Business Partner Certificates (Cross-Country): Managing customer certificates and their validity dates is a critical compliance task. You can now configure and maintain these certificates directly in the business partner master data, making the information readily available for processes like invoicing and payments. This improves compliance and reduces manual effort across all your regions of operation.

Business Partner Certificate

 

2) Expanded Scope Item Availability (Cross-Country): Our global family is always growing! We are thrilled to announce the availability of more scope items for dozens of SAP-delivered standard local versions. From Argentina to Australia, from Canada to Colombia, and from Singapore to South Africa, we are making it easier than ever to steer your business operations globally using SAPs standard, automated, and compliant processes.

AI campaigns

We’re excited to invite you to participate in the beta testing program for innovative new capabilities:

  1.  AI-Assisted Incoming Bills of Exchange Posting. Ready to get involved? Click the Register button at the top left to secure your spot. Together, let's make handling Bills of Exchange more efficient and straightforward!  
  2. EASYFIT: AI-Assisted Easy Entry of Treasury Financial Transactions: Get a preview of how AI can simplify the process of recording financial transactions in Treasury and Risk Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition while ensuring compliance with country specific regulations.

Delta localization information on new, changed or deleted features as well as legal changes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 for your country? Check the Help documentation country/region specific section What’s new

Check Localization Community

The 2602 release for Localization is more than just a list of features; it's our steadfast commitment to helping you navigate the complexities of global commerce with confidence and ease. We handle the local complexities so you can focus on what you do best: grow your business.

I hope you're as excited about these updates as I am! To learn more, I encourage you to explore the SAP Help Portal and join the conversation on the SAP Community.

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Localization as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎 Find all our assets 

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below: 

2602_Enablement-wheel

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Central Business Configuration 2601
entry.content:
With the latest release for SAP Central Business Configuration, exciting new features are available. This blog post will provide you with a concise overview of them.

Streamlined Sales Document Type Process

There’s a growing need to manage configuration content easily without having to deal with underlying complexity. Configuration templates are specifically designed to mask the complex semantics, enabling you to create configuration content through intuitive, user-friendly interfaces.

An exciting new feature concerning sales document types is available, allowing you to use a configuration template for order types. By copying configuration values from a standard order type, you can quickly create a custom sales order type. This feature can be used to create similar order types in SAP Central Business Configuration and deploy them to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for productive use and further configuration.

Another new aspect – even for early adopters of the previous release – is that when you create an order type, it’s initially saved as a draft. This means you can still delete it and start over if needed. Once you’re confident everything is correct, you simply confirm the order type and continue. This adds an extra layer of flexibility to the creation process.

Configure Standard Order Type activity

With this new feature, you can create and deploy variants of customer-specific sales order types with low customizing and maintenance efforts. It helps you streamline the sales document type process with tailor-made sales order types. Furthermore, it enables the Internal Sales Representative role to streamline operations by using a dedicated subset of preconfigured business process capabilities. Overall, this feature accelerates the configuration and implementation process. Looking ahead, we envision even more use cases for configuration templates beyond this first use case.

Smooth Access to Privacy Policies

You can now easily access the privacy and cookie statements in SAP Central Business Configuration anytime. Both documents are available in your profile under the Legal menu item, making them conveniently accessible whenever you need them.

Legal dropdown list in the profile

For a quick summary, check out this video about What's New in 2601. Don’t forget to also use the What’s New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal, which provides further details and links about all the recent changes.

That wraps up the new features this time. Did you like this blog? If so, please leave us a thumbs up at the top. Also, make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

Do you have any ideas on how to further enhance SAP Central Business Configuration? Go to the Customer Influence platform to submit an improvement request or vote for the ideas of others. This helps us understand your requirements even better and prioritize our work accordingly. We’re looking forward to your improvement requests.

entry.title: Finance in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Hello finance gurus! I’m thrilled to guide you through the exciting new finance innovations delivered in our 2602 release.

This update is packed with features designed to bring more intelligence, automation, and insight into your finance operations. We're continuing our journey to help you streamline core processes, enhance your analytical capabilities, and make faster, more data-driven decisions.

If you’re looking for a quick summary of the top Finance release highlights for version 2602 – check out my video.

For a full overview of all the release highlights, please see the table of contents below.

Let's dive into the highlights!

 

Video 1. Release Highlights for Finance in SAP Cloud ERP 2602

Table of contents

  • Embrace Intelligent Automation with AI
    • Accounting Accruals Agent
    • Joule for SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity
  • Deeper Insights with Next-Generation Analytics
    • Credit Line Analysis - Review Booklet
    • Actual Liquidity Analysis - Review Booklet 
    • Enhanced Reporting for Product and Service Margins
  • Streamlining Your Core Finance and Treasury Operations
    • New Foundation for Collection Processes
    • Flexible Partial Retirements in Asset Accounting
    • Innovations in Cash and Liquidity Management
      • Simplified Bank Account Master Data Distribution
      • More accurate short-term cash positioning
      • Introducing Cash Snapshots
  • Expanding the Financial Ecosystem for Complex Business Models
    • Universal Revenue Recognition (URR) for Solution Business
    • Innovations on the Horizon for SAP Green Ledger
      • Carbon tracking for projects and WBS elements
      • Accounting for Carbon Allowances (CBAM)
      • AI-Assisted Carbon Emission Analysis with Joule
  • Finance Intelligence Data Products for Business Data Cloud
  • Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series!
  • Find all our assets

This update highlights significant innovations in the following areas:


Embrace Intelligent Automation with AI

Artificial intelligence is no longer a future concept; it's a practical tool embedded directly into your core finance processes. In the 2602 release, we are introducing powerful AI capabilities to reduce manual effort and accelerate your operations.

<back to top>

Accounting Accruals Agent

Say goodbye to the time-consuming and manual process of calculating and posting accruals at period end. Our new agentic AI for accruals does the heavy lifting for you. It interprets your accounting policies, analyzes historical data, and generates accrual proposals. As a general ledger accountant, you are notified when proposals are ready, and you can quickly review and post them in the new Manage Accrual Proposals app, complete with detailed reasoning for each calculation. This significantly streamlines your accrual management and frees up your team for more strategic tasks.

This feature is going to be made generally available in SAP Cloud ERP 2602.1.

Video 2. Accounting Accruals Agent (opens in a new page)

 

Video 3. Accounting Accruals Agent - demo

Value Proposition

  • Significantly reduce time spent in calculating and preparing accrual postings at period end
  • Significantly reduce time reviewing the calculated accrual amounts and posting the journal entries
  • Streamline accrual management processes by transitioning from manual preparation and posting of general journal entries to an efficient, automated process.

Capabilities 

  • Automate Accruals with AI: Leverage agentic AI to generate accrual proposals for manual accruals by interpreting accounting policy documents, retrieving historical data, and performing the required calculations.
  • Instant Notification for Accountants: Notify the general ledger accountant when the accrual proposals are ready for review.
  • Streamlined Review Process: Enable the general ledger accountant to quickly and more simply review and post the proposed accrual journal entries in the new Manage Accrual Proposals app.
  • Transparent Calculations: Provide a detailed reasoning for the accrual amounts calculated by the AI agent and the proposed journal entries in the new Manage Accrual Proposals app.

For more information see: Discovery Center and Business Value 3-Pager

<back to top>

Joule for SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity

Finding the right information when you need it is crucial. We’ve integrated Joule, your AI copilot, with SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity. Now, you can ask questions about features, configuration, or integration scenarios in natural language. Joule searches the SAP Help Portal, analyzes the content, and provides you with clear, summarized answers, saving you time and streamlining access to relevant knowledge.

Picture 1. SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity: using Joule to access solution-related content

Value Proposition

  • Improve user satisfaction through the ability to express business requirements in natural language
  • Enable quicker access to user-enablement content summarized in a crisp answer
  • Save time by streamlining access to relevant information

Capabilities 

  • Quick overview: search and summarize SAP Help Portal documentation for a quick understanding of processes and features of SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity
  • Direct Access to Resources: provided links to original documentation for further deep-dive

For more information, see: Joule with SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity | SAP Help Portal

<back to top>

Deeper Insights with Next-Generation Analytics

The ability to analyze data from multiple dimensions is key to understanding business performance. With this release, we're expanding our powerful Review Booklet framework and enhancing our margin analysis reporting. Review Booklets represent the next generation of multidimensional analytics, creating a consistent, intuitive user experience.

<back to top>

New Review Booklets for Cash Management:

Credit Line Analysis - Review Booklet (F9064)

This new app enhances how you analyze credit line data with a superior user experience, more flexible querying, and embedded AI capabilities for generating insightful overviews. It provides the functionality of the Credit Line Analysis app (W0051) in the review booklet framework. It includes dedicated pages for utilization, transactions, and forecasts. You can generate several AI-assisted overviews for each of the tabs.

Picture 2. Credit Line Analysis - Review Booklet

Value Proposition

  • Improve the user experience by enhancing visualization and enabling faster response times
  • Enhance analysis capabilities with more-flexible querying for multidimensional insights
  • Provide an embedded AI capability

Capabilities 

  • Improved user experience: migrate the classic Credit Line Analysis app to an enhanced Review Booklet app for a better user experience
  • Extended capabilities: add multiple dedicated pages, including:
    • Credit Line Overview
    • Credit Line Utilization Report
    • Credit Line Analysis Transactions
    • Credit Line Analysis by Currency/Counterparty/Company Code
    • Forecast of Credit Line
  • AI-driven analytics

For more information, see: Credit Line Analysis – Review Booklet.

<back to top>

Actual Liquidity Analysis - Review Booklet (F7923)

Gain clear insights into your liquidity situation. This app presents your cash flow data in a standardized liquidity item hierarchy, showing opening and closing balances, highlighting unrealized FX effects, and summarizing past periods to identify where cash comes from and how it's used.

 

Video 4. Actual Liquidity Analysis

Value Proposition

  • Actual liquidity analysis of cash flows in bank accounts
  • Determining the sources and usages of cash flows
  • Empowered by Review Booklets

Capabilities 

  • Cash flow at a glance: view structured cash flow balance by fiscal year period in Transaction Currency and Display Currency.
  • Track FX Variances Easily: view Unrealized FX Difference for each fiscal year period.
  • Access to detailed information: Navigate to the Check Cash Flow Items app to analyze cash flow item details.

For more information, see: Actual Liquidity Analysis

<back to top>

Enhanced Reporting for Product and Service Margins (F9104)

The new version of the Product and Service Margins app provides a richer, more flexible multi-dimensional analysis experience. You can now analyze revenue, costs, and margins with support for multiple currencies (company code and global) and a new sales deduction measure, all while leveraging customizable charts and layouts for more intuitive reporting. 

Picture 3. Product and Service Margins app (F9104)

Value Proposition

  • Gain comprehensive profitability insights across various processes and objects
  • Enhance data visualization with intuitive charts for seamless reporting
  • Gain greater flexibility and relevance for diverse business needs through customizable reporting

Capabilities

  • Analysis on multiple levels: multidimensional analysis of profitability metrics across various objects such as service orders, sales orders, projects, solution orders, and provider contracts
  • Dynamic data visualization: visualization of profitability data interactively with customizable charts featuring series axis and export options
  • Tailored reporting: reports with custom fields to market segments

For more information, see: Product and Service Margins (New Version)

<back to top>

Streamlining Your Core Finance and Treasury Operations

Were constantly refining core processes to improve efficiency, control, and flexibility. The 2602 release brings significant enhancements across receivables, asset, and cash management.

<back to top>

New Foundation for Collection Processes

Improved functionality for receivables management (scope item 7MA) helps you collect receivables faster and minimize the impact of outstanding items. A prioritized customer worklist is the central starting point for agents, while automated processes and rule-based frameworks help automate team assignments and keep data in sync with AR accounting.

Picture 4. Process Worklist in Collections and Dispute Automation (F8832)

​Value Proposition

  • Minimizes operational and financial impact of outstanding receivables on operations and profitability
  • Swift collection processes leads to higher customer satisfaction and loyalty, positively impacting brand reputation
  • Actionable insights from analytics and real-time integration with accounts receivable help companies make informed decisions

​Capabilities

  • Central hub: the worklist with prioritized customers is the central starting point for collections agents.
  • Comprehensive account insights: collection accounts provide a comprehensive overview by combining key figures, information from master data, and related objects.
  • Collaborative collection tools: use tasks and activities as collaborative tools to facilitate internal collection workflows.
  • Track customer agreements: Keep track of agreements with your customers by recording promises to pay and disputes.
  • Tailor customer prioritization: use provided rules to prioritize customers according to your requirements.
  • Automate team assignments: apply the rules framework to automate team assignments and set business blocks.
  • Sync processes automatically: automated background processes keep data and organizational structures in sync with accounts receivable accounting.

For more information, see: Collections and Dispute Automation

<back to top>


Flexible Partial Retirements in Asset Accounting

You now have more control over partial asset retirements. In the Post Asset Retirement app (F7107), you can choose a processing sequence that mirrors a LIFO (Last In, First Out) or FIFO (First In, First Out) method, deciding whether to retire balances from the current year first or from prior years first.

Picture 5. Post Asset Retirements (F7107)

​Value Proposition

  • Decide whether you want to retire older or newer asset balances first
  • Define the processing sequence for partial retirements by choosing a LIFO or FIFO-like option

​Capabilities

  • Based on the chosen option, the system considers the respective balance amounts from the different fiscal year when making the retirement posting. The following new options are now available:
    • Current Year First, Then Prior Years: The system first considers balance amounts resulting from acquisitions of the current year, then ones from previous years. This option corresponds to the LIFO (Last In First Out) method in inventory accounting.
    • Prior Years First, Then Current Year: The system first considers balance amounts of previous years, then ones from the current year. This option corresponds to the FIFO (First In First Out) method in inventory accounting.

For more information, see: Processing Sequence Depending on the Year of Acquisition

<back to top>


Innovations in Cash and Liquidity Management

Simplified Bank Account Master Data Distribution

For organizations using a Treasury Workstation, you can now configure system groups to streamline the distribution of bank account master data, ensuring only relevant information is replicated to target systems.

Picture 6. System groups for bank accounts

​Value Proposition

  • Centralized management offers a unified view and easier maintenance of bank account distribution across systems.
  • Significantly reduces complexity in system mapping configurations through BAdIs in receiver systems, thereby reducing IT involvement and associated costs
  • Enhanced efficiency with automated house bank account conversion.
  • Flexibility is provided through configuration for direct copying or conversion of remote house bank accounts, tailored to specific business needs.

​Capabilities 

  • Simplified system management: system group configuration for easy management of systems in the Treasury Workstation setup
  • Targeted data replication: during data replication, the system automatically identifies and sends only the bank accounts that are relevant to a specific remote system by filtering based on house bank account connectivity.
  • Seamless account conversion: automatically converts house bank accounts from remote systems to operative ones when they are replicated to the target systems.
  • Account details: additional attributes, such as general ledger account numbers, are provided for remote house bank accounts.

For more information, see: System Groups for Bank Account Master Data Distribution.

<back to top>

More accurate short-term cash positioning

The Short-Term Cash Positioning app now supports the opening available balance from intraday bank statements, providing a more accurate starting cash position for better-informed treasury decisions.

Picture 7. Short-term cash positioning

​Value Proposition

  • A  more accurate starting cash position by using the opening available balance to reflect the funds can be spent at the beginning of the banking day.
  • Better-informed treasury decisions based on more up-to-date fund availability rather than relying on the previous day's closing balances
  • A seamless and reliable cash position is achieved by using intraday bank statements as the source of opening balances, with a built-in fallback to the prior day's closing balance when intraday bank statements are not available.

​Capabilities

  • New option to use opening available balances as the source of opening balances in the Define Cash Position Profiles app.
  • Consumption of opening available balances in the Short-Term Cash Positioning app.
  • Fallback mechanism that uses end-of-day balances when intraday bank statements are not available.

For more information, see: Integrating Intraday Opening Available Balances in Short-Term Cash Positioning.

Introducing Cash Snapshots

With the new Manage Cash Snapshots app (F8962), you can capture a snapshot of your cash-related data (like cash flows and balances) at a specific timestamp. This allows you to track and analyze historical cash data, review past decisions, and identify late-posting transactions.

Picture 8. Manage Cash Snapshots app (F8962)

​Value Proposition

  • Capture cash position snapshots for future review and decision justification.
  • Help users to identify late-posting transactions and discrepancies.

​Capabilities

  • Capture cash snapshots manually or via scheduled jobs.
  • Centrally manage cash snapshots in one app.
  • Delete cash snapshots that are no longer needed.
  • Load captured snapshots for analysis using the Short-Term Cash Positioning app.

For more information, see: New Apps for Cash Snapshots and Cash Snapshots in Short-Term Cash Positioning.

<back to top>

Expanding the Financial Ecosystem for Complex Business Models

As business models evolve, so must your financial software. This release adds critical support for solution-oriented business and enhances data accessibility.

<back to top>

Universal Revenue Recognition (URR) for Solution Business

URR now supports solution orders that combine sales orders, customer projects, and service or subscription contracts. This enables you to automate revenue recognition for these complex multi-element arrangements, ensuring IFRS 15 compliance and providing powerful analytic reporting for a complete accounting perspective.

 

Video 5. Universal Revenue Recognition (URR) for Solution Business

​Value Proposition

  • Enable customers to automate revenue recognition for solution orders to adhere to IFRS15 compliance rules

​Capabilities

  • Complex arrangements possible: enable multi-element arrangement across the various item types of a solution order
  • Allocation flexibility: for the allocation, use a standalone selling price for sales, service contracts, subscriptions, and fixed-price customer project items
  • Insightful revenue analytics: provide analytic reporting for solution business from the accounting perspective, such as waterfall reporting for revenue forecasts and allocation impact

For more information, see: Universal Revenue Recognition for Bundling with Solution Orders | SAP Help Portal.

<back to top>

Innovations on the Horizon for SAP Green Ledger

The increasing demand for transparency in environmental impact reporting requires a new level of financial and non-financial data integration. The SAP Green Ledger is our answer, embedding carbon accounting directly into the core of your financial system. Heres a look at the powerful innovations on our roadmap that will redefine how you manage sustainability.

<back to top>

Carbon tracking for projects and WBS elements

To truly understand your carbon footprint, you need to track emissions at a granular level. We've enhanced the Green Ledger to allow for the posting and analysis of carbon quantities directly on projects and WBS elements. This will give you profound insights into the emissions generated by specific services, investments, and customer-facing projects.

 

Video 6. Carbon tracking for projects and WBS elements

Value Proposition: Improve decision-making by understanding the climate impact of your projects, enable accurate reporting on services delivered to customers, and gain a holistic view of emissions from all business activities.
Capabilities to Look Forward To: Replicating project structures, collecting carbon quantities on WBS elements, and analyzing this data seamlessly within your finance system.

For more information, see Import Master Data.

<back to top>

Accounting for Carbon Allowances (CBAM)

Regulatory requirements are evolving, and starting in 2026, the EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) will mandate that companies account for liabilities from embedded carbon in imported goods. To ensure you are prepared, we are developing capabilities for accounting for carbon allowances within a dedicated carbon journal.

 

Video 7. Accounting for Carbon Allowances (CBAM)

Value Proposition: This will help you comply with EU Regulation 2023/956 and other greenhouse gas accounting rules (like US GAAP and IFRS guidance), ensuring auditability and sufficient coverage of emissions with allowances.
Capabilities to Look Forward To: Tracking embedded carbon in imported goods, accounting for emission quantities and allowances, and preparing the necessary financial postings for both gross and net models.

For more information, see Carbon Certificate Management.​

<back to top>

AI-Assisted Carbon Emission Analysis with Joule

Data is only as valuable as the insights you can derive from it. We are bringing the power of our generative AI assistant, Joule, to the Green Ledger. With AI-assisted carbon emission analysis, you'll be able to generate charts and visualizations from your carbon data using simple conversational prompts.

 

Video 8. AI-Assisted Carbon Emission Analysis with Joule

Value Proposition: Reduce the time and effort needed for carbon reporting, gain instant insights to support reduction strategies, and make sophisticated data analysis accessible to a broader range of users.

Capabilities to Look Forward To: Generating various chart types by simply asking Joule, analyzing dimensions like GHG scope and category, and even asking follow-up questions for deeper analysis.

For more information, see SAP Green Ledger, carbon emission analysis in SAP Discovery Center .

<back to top>

Finance Intelligence Data Products for Business Data Cloud

We are delivering over 75 primary data products for Finance in the Business Data Cloud. These building blocks covering areas like accounting, financial close, cash, and group reporting ensure your finance data is available to build analytical apps with near real-time insights and make data-driven decisions.
<back to top>

This is just a glimpse of the many innovations in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2602 release. Our goal is to empower you with the tools you need to run an intelligent, efficient, and agile finance organization.

I encourage you to explore these features further by visiting the linked resources in the SAP Help Portal and checking out the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Artificial Intelligence as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

<back to top>

🔎 Find all our assets 

Do you want to learn more about the main innovations in different lines of business in the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release? The release 2602 showcases more 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

Enablement

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below: 

<back to top>

Find all enablement assets and update blog post for Finance: Finance for SAP S/4HANA Cloud – The Collection

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Also, visit the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow me on LinkedIn and stay up-to-date to our latest enablement assets: Alina Keilbach | LinkedIn

entry.title: Integration: SAP SuccessFactors with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Integration Architecture

The integration leverages SAP Master Data Integration (MDI) service running on SAP Business Technology Platform as the central hub for data replication between systems .

Key components include:

  • SAP Master Data Integration Service: Acts as the middleware for data transformation and routing
  • SAP Integration Suite: Provides cloud integration capabilities and pre-packaged integration content
  • Communication Scenarios: Enable secure data exchange between systems
  • Web Services and APIs: Support real-time and batch data synchronization

Integration Methods

  1. SAP Delivered Integrations

    • Full end-to-end integration processes for specific hybrid cloud scenarios
    • Pre-configured best practices with guided configuration and onboarding 
    • Scope item JB1 for SuccessFactors Employee Central integration 

  2. Template-based Integrations

    • Simplified integration templates primarily for standard scenarios
    • Leverages SAP Integration Suite with pre-built integration flows 

  3. Customer Driven Integrations

    • Custom integrations using SAP Integration Suite and APIs
    • Supports specific business requirements beyond standard scenarios 

Data Flow Scenarios

Scenario Direction Data Objects Frequency
Employee Master DataSF → S/4HANAPersonnel data, organizational assignmentsReal-time/Scheduled
Organizational StructureSF → S/4HANACompany codes, departments, positionsReal-time/Scheduled
Cost Center DataS/4HANA → SFCost center master dataScheduled
Payroll ResultsSF → S/4HANAPayroll posting documentsAfter payroll run
Availability DataSF → S/4HANAEmployee availability informationReal-time

Configuration Steps

  1. Pre-requisites

    • SAP Business Technology Platform subscription
    • Communication user setup in both systems
    • Required authorizations and certificates 

  2. SuccessFactors Configuration

    • Create communication users for MDI integration
    • Configure OAuth connections for secure authentication
    • Set up business scenarios for data replication 
    • Enable address mapping and field mappings

  3. S/4HANA Public Cloud Configuration

    • Activate communication scenarios (e.g., SAP_COM_0193 for Employee Central integration)
    • Create communication systems and arrangements 
    • Configure destinations for data flow
    • Set up Business Integration Builder for infotype mapping

Key Integration Points

  • Employee Master Data: Replicates personnel information, employment details, and personal data from Employee Central to S/4HANA for finance and reporting purposes 
  • Organizational Structure: Synchronizes company codes, cost centers, departments, and positions to maintain consistent organizational hierarchy
  • Time Management: Integrates time data and availability information for workforce planning and scheduling 
  • Payroll Data: Transfers payroll results from SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll to General Ledger Accounting in S/4HANA 

Technical Considerations

  • Authentication: OAuth 2.0 and certificate-based authentication for secure communication
  • Data Mapping: Field mappings defined in SAP Note 3078790 for New Business Partner Model 
  • Error Handling: Data Replication Monitor in Employee Central provides status tracking and error resolution 
  • Performance: Maximum 2000 line items per payroll posting document 

Monitoring & Maintenance

  • Data Replication Monitor: Available in Employee Central for monitoring replication status and re-running failed jobs 
  • Integration Monitoring: SAP Integration Suite provides comprehensive monitoring capabilities
  • Query Administration: S/4HANA maintains query administration tables for tracking integration requests 
  • Application Logs: Detailed logging for troubleshooting and audit purposes

Best Practices

  • Fit-to-Standard Approach: Limit customizations to accelerate implementation and simplify ongoing maintenance 
  • Master Data Governance: Establish clear data ownership with SuccessFactors as the leading system for employees 
  • Security: Implement proper authentication mechanisms and follow SAP security guidelines
  • Testing: Thoroughly test integration scenarios in development environments before production deployment
  • Documentation: Maintain comprehensive documentation of field mappings and custom configurations

entry.title: Using a Lookup Table for Profit Center Determination in Service Contracts
entry.content:
With SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 we enabled a couple of new fields to be used for profit center determination in service contracts: 

  • Bill-to Party 
  • Sold-to Party 
  • Ship-to Party 

Using these new fields can lead to a lot of manual input during period-end closing, because a lot of values of these new fields determine the same profit center. To reduce the effort, I recommend using a lookup table, which is easier to maintain than maintaining every single customer you have. So, here is a guide on how to effectively use a table lookup in the Manage Substitution/Validation Rules – Service Documents app to determine profit centers for service contracts. 

Before we can get down to the nitty-gritty, let’s first clarify some of the prerequisites and terminology involved. 

Prerequisites 

Before you implement the Profit Center Derivation, you need to check the profit center determination access sequence described in the following link for the Help Portal page: 
Profit Center Determination for  Service Transactions 

Please make sure you have access to the following apps: 

  • Custom Business Objects (F1712) 
  • Manage Substitution/Valdiation Rules – Service Documents (F7818) 

The above-mentioned apps are SAP pre-delivered under the following roles: 

  • SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC 
  • SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT 

What are Substitution Rules? 

Substitution rules signify which profit center, costing sheet, functional area, settlement profile or costing variant is applied. 

When you create a service transaction, the system uses the conditions that you have defined in the substitution rule to derive the available target field for the item. When you make any changes to the service transaction item, the system automatically redetermines the profit center, costing sheet, functional area, settlement profile, or costing variant using the conditions which were defined for the item. (Source: SAP Help) 

How and When to Use Substitution Rules? 

You create a substitution rule to determine the profit center based on the material (service product) and the plant. The system uses the plant-dependent product master to determine the profit center. If you change the product, the system redetermines the profit center. From the 2502 release on, you can derive profit centers in service contracts with new fields, such as Sold-to-Party, Ship-to-Party, or Bill-to-Party using the app Manage Substitution/Validation Rules – Service Documents. 

Precondition: 

You use the precondition section to identify a field with a certain value to be used for substitution.  

Substitution: 

You use the substitution section to determine the field value to be substituted with a new field value. 

What is a lookup table? 

In the app Custom Business Objects, you create a new business object with a table, in which you can find both, the precondition value for the source field and the substitution value for the target field, which will receive the value found in the lookup table associated with the precondition field. 

How to Use the Lookup Table in the Substitution Tool 

Disclaimer: In this blog, for better readability, we will use the Sold-to Party field as an example. You can do the same process for the Ship-to Party or Bill-to Party fields. 

So, let’s get into it. 

To create a lookup table to use in the profit center derivation, you first have to create a new business object with a table with the desired fields in the Custom Business Objects app. 

In the Create Custom Business Objects in SAP S/4HANA Cloud tutorial group (requires partner/customer license) it shows how to create a custom business object and generate a user interface in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

The table has to contain the source field, in this case the Sold-to Party (SoldToParty) and the target field, which is the Profit Center (ProfitCenter). After adding the fields, you must check the “User Interface” checkbox and publish the business object to be able to use it in the Manage Substitution/Validation Rules – Service Documents app. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

After publishing the business object, you must add the tile, which you created by checking the “User Interface” checkbox, to your business catalog. To find out how to do that, see How to Add Custom Catalog Extensions | SAP Help Portal. 

You can now open the tile from your Fiori Launchpad and add values for the fields you added. 

After that, you can now use this lookup table in the Manage Substitution/Validation Rules – Service Documents app. 

In the Manage Substitution/Validation Rules – Service Documents app, you create the substitution rule in the following way: 

Use the Precondition to specify the source field to be used for substitution. For this case, we are using the Sold-to Party. Make sure you set the Operator to “Is not empty”. 

After that, you specify the value and the target field to be used in the substitution. To do so, set the Target Field to Profit Center and use the Substitution Type “Table Lookup”. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

To find the correct value to be substituted, you have to specify the data source and the condition, which you can do using the Source field. To do so, open the value help of the Source field and specify a Data Source and the Source Field. As the Data Source, select the lookup table you created earlier in the Custom Business Objects app. As the Source Field, select the Identifier of the Profit Center field that you used in the lookup table. 

You can now set the conditions that specify when the field will be substituted. To do so, in the Conditions field, select the Identifier of the Sold-to Party you used in the lookup table. As the Operator, you have to use “Equal to FieldFunction”. In the Value field, you then have to select the Identifier of the Sold-to Party (SoldToParty) field in the Service Document. 

You can check the “Overwrite” checkbox to make sure the value you determined for the profit center overwrites any previously filled-in value in the target field. 

After you have activated the rule, the system checks whether the Sold-to Party field filled in the Service Document equals the Sold-to Party field in the lookup table. If this is true, the corresponding profit center from the lookup table will be used in the Service Contract.  

This should save you some time during profit center determination, and if you want to look further, here are some resources from SAP Help: 

  • Profit Center Determination for Service Transactions 
  • Manage Substitution/Validation Rules - Service Documents 
  • Creating Custom Business Objects | SAP Help Portal 
  • Creating Extension Items and Transporting Them | SAP Help Portal 
  • Create Custom Business Objects in S/4HANA Cloud | SAP Tutorials
